name: Skill-description hygiene: trigger-only fix (commit, worktree-setup) + scope-keyword-check hook skill-ref
description: Three small agent-instruction correctness fixes: two skill descriptions carry workflow narration (violating the trigger-only rule), and a hook references a non-existent skill.
acceptanceCriteria: F-039: .agents/skills/commit/SKILL.md description becomes trigger-only (remove the 'Thin overlay over /zdx-core:commit...' workflow clause). F-040: .agents/skills/worktree-setup/SKILL.md description becomes trigger-only (remove the 'Creates a git worktree at .worktrees/...' clause). F-042: .claude/hooks/scope-keyword-check.sh no longer references the non-existent 'deep-scope-understanding' skill — default fix (a): make the hook self-contained (embed the enumeration inline); hook still exits 0 on a non-matching prompt. Run pnpm sync-skills so .claude/skills mirrors the masters.
bodyText:
Findings
F-039 — .agents/skills/commit/SKILL.md description ends with "Thin overlay over the global /zdx-core:commit primitive — it adds only EduAgent's message conventions." — workflow narration, not a trigger condition.
F-040 — .agents/skills/worktree-setup/SKILL.md description prepends "Creates a git worktree at .worktrees/<branch>/ from origin/main, runs pnpm install, syncs secrets." before the trigger.
F-042 — .claude/hooks/scope-keyword-check.sh references a deep-scope-understanding skill that does not exist under .agents/skills/ or .claude/skills/.
Fix
Trigger-only descriptions per AGENTS.md §Skill Authoring (description: = when to use, start with "Use when …"). For F-042, default to option (a): self-contained hook (no skill ref). Edit masters under .agents/skills/, then pnpm sync-skills.
Note
F-038 (code-review / thermo-nuclear skill descriptions) is moot — those skills were removed; nothing to fix. Single PR; pre-commit + sync-skills check covers it.
